extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu attacked with 36km remaining, bridged from behind after being caught, won from group sprint on Cauberg finish.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL Mathieu's 36km solo attack absorbed by chasers, but energy reserves sufficient to win final sprint anyway.
FUGLSANG Jakob and KWIATKOWSKI Michał bridging together created numerical disadvantage that paradoxically allowed regrouping before Cauberg.
CLARKE Simon marking VAN DER POEL in final 400m instead of initiating own sprint cost potential victory.
Cauberg crosswind from final turn favored right-hand positioning; leaders hesitation in final 200m allowed chasers back into contention.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: demonstrated ability to recover from failed long-range effort and still win tactical sprint finish
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: responded immediately to attacks at 36km, showing form to bridge significant gaps late-race
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: bridged effectively with Kwiatkowski but unable to capitalize in final sprint from reduced group
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: chase work successful but sprint positioning inadequate despite being in winning move
rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: negative: finished second despite perfect position in final 200m, suggesting hesitation or misjudged timing cost victory
rider_signals.DE MARCHI Alessandro: positive: survived from early breakaway to finish sixth, indicating exceptional diesel endurance for Amstel distance
